content: Here's my code with a tricky edge case. The string contains backticks: `let x = "\`hello\`";` and I need to handle it.

That URL has parens in the query string. The extractor should capture the whole URL up to but not including the trailing punctuation. Try also: http://localhost:8080/api/v1/users/123 and ftp://not-http-so-skip.example.com — only the http(s) ones should be picked up.

Also testing emoji handling: 🥤 → 🥵 ContextGuard, and a unicode path: src/café/résumé.ts — should still parse as a file_path.

Decided: we will keep the regex conservative. APPROVED. TODO: add more tests.
